concept: Concentration / Amount of an Agent per Stated Medium
description: The amount of a stated substance, material, organism, or agent relative to a stated quantity of medium, volume, mass, area, or other denominator. A concentration claim requires its agent, basis, unit, method, and source when they matter. The word does not mean total amount, technical load, toxicity, exposure, dose, purity, a mental act of attention, or proof that a threshold has been exceeded.
sound_symbolism: Grounded 'no' establishes the medium or denominator, 'mi' gathers the stated agent within it, and precise 'ki' closes the relative measurement. The compact form holds amount against medium rather than presenting an isolated number.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun commonly possessed by the agent or medium: 'ha phialu ha hisophi nomiki phelu.' (This water has this contaminant concentration.) Preserve exact values and units in a separate source record. Distinct from 'masue' (measure), 'takori' (load), total quantity, and a psychological concentration of attention.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Concentration makes environmental claims more precise while preventing a visible substance or large total amount from replacing an actual ratio and source.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The concept is explicitly relational: an amount has ecological meaning only against a medium, scale, condition, and interpretation.

tags.cognition: a ratio whose agent, denominator, method, and source shape interpretation
tags.physical: an amount of matter, organisms, or another agent per stated medium
